            cell [e158] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Complete: { \"content\": \"In the context of SPARTA technique DE-0003.09 (System Clock for Evasion), both CM0034 (Monitor Critical Telemetry Points) and CM0042 (Robust Fault Management) are controls that contribute to detecting and mitigating the technique. As per primary QRA evidence, CM0034 continuously monitors telemetry frames for anomalies in timing information, thereby identifying possible clock manipulation. CM0042 also monitors telemetry frame timing for anomalies and, when discrepancies are detected, can initiate predefined responses such as isolating subsystems or reverting to a secure state; additionally, it ensures the fault management system itself cannot be exploited as part of an attack. The relationship between these controls is complementary: CM0034 provides the detection of timing anomalies, while CM0042 ensures that the fault management system can respond appropriately and remains secure from exploitation. Together, they form a layered defense against DE-0003.09, with monitoring from CM0034 and response/protection from CM0042.\", \"citations\": [\"DE-0003.09\", \"CM0034\", \"CM0042\"], \"confidence\": \"medium\", \"caveats\": [\"The evidence does not explicitly describe the direct relationship between CM0034 and CM0042; the answer is synthesized from their individual roles in addressing DE-0003.09 as presented in the primary QRA evidence.\"] }" [e159] [cursor=pointer]
